result.question:
For the WAGER project we build executable "worlds" (test scenarios) that each expose a specific failure mode of investigative/scientific JUDGMENT in AI/LLM agents (not sloppiness — judgment: has all the info in context and still reasons wrong). We already have a catalog of 7 vice-families. We now need CONCRETE, DOCUMENTED CASES AND EXAMPLES of each vice — real episodes where it actually happened — and CRUCIALLY we need to surface the DIFFERENT UNDERLYING STRUCTURES each vice can take, not merely different surface domains.

THE KEY POINT (please honor it): a single vice arises through several genuinely DIFFERENT structural situations/mechanisms — not one trap in different costumes. Example, for "failure to revise beliefs against contradicting evidence", the distinct STRUCTURES are: (a) contradicting evidence arriving mid-investigation that must be incorporated; (b) a hypothesis that is a special case embedded in the true rule, so positive/confirming tests can NEVER falsify it (Wason 2-4-6 / Klayman-Ha); (c) evidence that is later retracted or discredited yet the belief persists (belief perseverance, e.g. Anderson-Lepper, Apollo moon-rock scientists); (d) anchoring on the first estimate. These are structurally different traps. We want that STRUCTURAL variety FOR EACH vice, because a portfolio of worlds that all share one structure would let a model trained on them OVERFIT to that structure instead of learning the underlying judgment skill.

The 7 vices:
1. Failure to update beliefs / confirmation bias / belief perseverance / anchoring.
2. Attention misallocation / rabbit-holing / failure to stop or pivot / sunk-cost persistence.
3. Failure to verify / overclaiming / fabricated precision / accepting a cheap-but-false answer.
4. Failure to infer hidden/latent structure / retreat to familiar textbook models / missing the imaginative leap.
5. Loss of constraints or goal over long horizons.
6. Guessing instead of investigating / not asking / not registering mid-way information.
7. Causal-reasoning failures: confounding, correlation-vs-causation, collider/selection bias, failing to intervene when observation is insufficient.

For EACH vice, find CONCRETE CASES spanning as many DIFFERENT STRUCTURAL SETUPS and DIFFERENT FIELDS as possible. Draw from: history of science (famous errors, discoveries missed/delayed, e.g. N-rays, cold fusion, the delayed acceptance of Helicobacter/ulcers, continental drift, the missed ozone hole); in-vivo/naturalistic studies of working scientists (Dunbar); cognitive-science experimental paradigms; metascience case studies (p-hacking, forking paths in real papers); documented AI-agent/LLM failure traces from benchmarks; and case studies from medicine, physics, biology, economics, and machine learning.

For each case report: (a) which vice it exemplifies; (b) the FIELD/scenario; (c) the underlying STRUCTURE — what specifically made the vice the losing move (the mechanism), described abstractly enough that it could be rebuilt as a synthetic world; (d) the source (author/paper/case, year); (e) a short label for the STRUCTURAL TYPE, so cases of the same vice can be clustered by structure. Group by vice; within each vice, CLUSTER BY STRUCTURAL TYPE and explicitly note how many distinct structures were found. Prioritize STRUCTURAL DIVERSITY and concrete, verifiable, ideally quantified cases over generic commentary.
result.summary: The 23 verified claims map unevenly onto the seven vices but strongly confirm the core premise: a single vice fractures into genuinely different structural traps, not one trap in different costumes. Vice #1 (belief updating) is by far the best-supported, with at least eight distinct rebuildable structures — from the formal falsifiability-geometry of Klayman-Ha (the subset/embedded case where positive tests can never falsify, and its mirror superset case where negative tests can never falsify), through laboratory belief-perseverance-after-total-discrediting and anchoring-by-insufficient-adjustment, to four naturalistic history-of-science structures (confirmation-seeking-not-falsification, motte-and-bailey unfalsifiability, corrupt validating reference, asymmetric double standard) — plus two orthogonal taxonomic axes (six processing forms; five pipeline stages) that systematize the space. Vice #6 (guessing instead of investigating) is the strongest AI-native cluster with four quantified LLM-benchmark structures (recognition-vs-behavior dissociation, injected-context-suppressed inquiry, incompatible-hypothesis sampling, non-adaptive questioning); Vice #3 (p-hacking) is quantified (5% to 60.7% false positives) and decomposes into four sub-mechanisms; Vice #7 (confounding) is documented across four water-science episodes plus a corrupt-reference variant. Critically, Vices #4 (hidden/latent structure, imaginative leap) and #5 (long-horizon goal/constraint loss) produced NO confirmed cases, and Vice #2 rests on a single historical episode — these are the portfolio's structural blind spots requiring dedicated follow-up.

findings.claim: Vice #1 (failure to update beliefs / confirmation bias / belief perseverance / anchoring) is the richest vice, documented across at least EIGHT structurally distinct rebuildable traps (nine counting a cross-listed variant), plus two orthogonal taxonomic axes that systematize the space — spanning formal falsifiability-geometry, laboratory perseverance/anchoring, and naturalistic history-of-science.
findings.vote: 3-0 on the formal/lab/history structures [0][1][4][5][11][12][14][15][16][17][20]; 2-1 on the two taxonomy claims [2][3]
findings.evidence: Distinct structural types (short label -> mechanism -> case/source): (1a) SUBSET/EMBEDDED-HYPOTHESIS -> H is a subset of the true target T, so positive/confirming tests can NEVER falsify; only negative tests are diagnostic (Wason 2-4-6). Klayman & Ha 1987, Psych Review 94(2):211-228 [claim 0]. This is exactly structure (b) from the question. (1b) SUPERSET/SURROUNDING-HYPOTHESIS -> the MIRROR: H is too broad and surrounds T, so now ONLY positive tests can falsify and negative testing misleads; escape behavior is reversed vs 1a (Klayman-Ha Fig 3). Same paper [claim 1]. A model overfit to 'seek disconfirming tests' is systematically misled here. (1c) BELIEF-PERSEVERANCE-AFTER-TOTAL-DISCREDITING -> a theory induced from evidence survives explicit debriefing that the founding case studies were completely fictitious; beliefs barely move (firefighter risk-taking study). Anderson, Lepper & Ross 1980, JPSP 39:1037-1049 [claim 4]. This is structure (c). Its distinguishing MECHANISM is self-generated causal explanation: in Exp 2, subjects in the NO-explanation condition DID update after debriefing, but those who wrote a causal explanation did not — so being asked to explain WHY immunizes the belief (mechanistically different from anchoring or mid-stream contradiction) [claim 5]. (1d) ANCHORING / INSUFFICIENT-ADJUSTMENT -> start from a reference value and stop short while adjusting toward truth; an effortful Type-2 process for at least some subjects. Quantified in a within-subject self-generated-reference design (each participant's own prior estimate is the anchor, external anchors at 20/40/60/80% distance): final estimates pulled 21.4% of the initial value toward the anchor, 95% CI [20.3, 22.5]. Teovanovic 2019, Europe's J Psych (PMC6396698) [claims 11,12]. This is structure (d). (1e) CONFIRMATION-SEEKING-NOT-FALSIFICATION (behavioral/motivational) -> researchers 'latch onto' a belief then run only confirmatory tests and decline to try to falsify. Elton & Spencer 2020 'Pathological Water Science' (arXiv 2010.07287) [claim 14]. (1f) MOTTE-AND-BAILEY / ILL-DEFINED-TARGET -> the phenomenon is imprecisely defined so a proponent asserts a strong claim ('hot water freezes faster than cold'), retreats to a trivial weak claim under pressure, then reconflates them — rendering the belief effectively unfalsifiable; the TARGET itself is ill-defined so no evidence can pin it down (structurally different from confounding). Same paper [claim 15]. (1g) CORRUPT-VALIDATING-REFERENCE -> a genuine anomaly (low satellite ozone) appears to be an instrument artifact because the ground-truth reference used to validate it (South Pole Dobson, ~2x too high) is ITSELF wrong (operator error, later retracted); the anchor spuriously confirms the wrong conclusion. Bhartia & McPeters 2018, C.R. Geoscience 350 (the NASA TOMS/SBUV principals) [claims 16,17]. Note: the authors REBUT the popular 'QC filter silently auto-deleted the discovery' myth; the surviving structure is 'distrust a flagged anomaly + demand corroboration + discount it', distinct from 'blind filtering'. (1h) ASYMMETRIC-DOUBLE-STANDARD-BY-PRIOR-COMMITMENT -> the same evidentiary bar is applied strictly to the disfavored hypothesis and leniently to the incumbent (geologists demanded a mechanism from continental drift while exempting their own fixist land-bridges, which equally lacked one); root diagnosed as bias toward fixist frameworks, not fair standards. Seselja & Weber 2012, Stud. Hist. Phil. Sci. 43(1):147-159 [claim 20]. (1i, cross-listed with Vice #2) SINGLE-GAP-VETO -> one unresolved sub-problem (Wegener's driving forces ~1,000,000x too weak, per Jeffreys/Le Grand) allowed to override a preponderance of converging evidence (fossils, matching geology, paleoclimate). Same paper [claim 19]. TWO ORTHOGONAL TAXONOMIC AXES that span the structure space: (i) SIX processing FORMS of belief-consistent information processing — positive testing, selective exposure/congeniality bias, biased assimilation, motivated skepticism, belief perseverance, subtyping (Oeberst & Imhoff 2023, Perspectives on Psych Science, PMC10623627) [claim 2]; (ii) FIVE pipeline STAGES where the vice bites — attention, perception, evaluation, reconstruction/memory, search for new information [claim 3]. NOTE a sub-gap even within this well-covered vice: the question's structure (a) 'contradicting evidence arriving mid-investigation that must be incorporated' is only weakly represented here, surfacing mainly via LLM history-incorporation failures classed under Vice #6.

findings.claim: Vice #2 (attention misallocation / rabbit-holing / failure to stop-or-pivot / sunk-cost persistence) has THIN confirmed coverage: only ~2 rebuildable structures survived, both from a single historical episode (continental drift) and a single source; no sunk-cost, rabbit-holing, or LLM agentic-loop case was confirmed.
findings.vote: underlying claims 3-0 each [18][19], but single-source and the vice-2 mapping is partly interpretive
findings.evidence: Structural types found: (2a) ACCEPTANCE-VS-PURSUIT-CONFLATION -> a hypothesis correctly judged 'not yet proven' is wrongly dropped from the ACTIVE INVESTIGATION set; rejecting-for-acceptance and pursuing are compatible stances, and the vice is treating 'unproven' as 'not worth exploring'. Seselja & Weber 2012 argue it was rational to reject drift's full acceptance but IRRATIONAL to reject its pursuit as unworthy [claim 18]. Rebuildable as: a correct 'unproven' verdict triggers an incorrect 'stop investigating' action. (2b) SINGLE-GAP-VETO -> one unsolved sub-problem vetoes a theory with strong converging support (the ~1,000,000x-too-weak driving forces overriding fossils/geology/paleoclimate) [claim 19]; cross-listed with Vice #1 because it is also a belief-weighting failure (over-weighting one anomaly). Both come from Seselja & Weber 2012, Stud. Hist. Phil. Sci. 43(1):147-159. LIMITATION: this is a single episode and single peer-reviewed source; classic Vice-#2 structures (sunk-cost escalation, rabbit-holing/tunnel-vision, failure to abandon a dead line, budget/attention misallocation over a long task) did NOT produce a confirmed case in this evidence set — a priority follow-up target.

findings.claim: Vice #3 (failure to verify / overclaiming / accepting a cheap-but-false answer) is anchored by the p-hacking / garden-of-forking-paths structure, whose 'losing move' is quantified (nominal 5% false-positive rate raised to 60.7% when all four researcher degrees of freedom are exploited) and which decomposes into FOUR structurally distinct sub-mechanisms.
findings.vote: 3-0 on the quantified result [21]; 2-1 on the four-mechanism decomposition [22] (core verbatim-verified)
findings.evidence: QUANTIFIED LOSING MOVE: via 15,000-run Monte Carlo simulation, a researcher exploiting all four common 'researcher degrees of freedom' at once raises the false-positive rate from 5% to 60.7% at p<.05 — making a false 'significant' result MORE LIKELY THAN NOT. Simmons, Nelson & Simonsohn 2011, 'False-Positive Psychology', Psychological Science 22(11):1359-1366 (Table 1, exact figure 60.7%) [claim 21]. FOUR DISTINCT SUB-STRUCTURES (each a separate world setup with different affordances and different correct corrections): (3a) FLEXIBILITY-IN-DEPENDENT-VARIABLES (pick the DV that reaches significance); (3b) OPTIONAL-STOPPING / SAMPLE-SIZE flexibility (add observations after peeking); (3c) COVARIATE flexibility (add/drop controls to cross the threshold); (3d) CONDITION-SUBSET reporting (report only the arms that 'worked'). Same paper [claim 22]. Maps to Vice #3 (overclaiming / accepting a cheap-but-false answer) and to Vice #6 (choosing the answer that 'works' instead of investigating truth). CAVEAT: the four sub-mechanisms share a 'pick-the-winner uncorrected multiplicity' family (Gelman-Loken forking paths), so at coarse granularity they could collapse toward ~2 structures (optional-stopping vs static-multiplicity); for world-building they remain genuinely different decision points.

findings.claim: Vice #6 (guessing instead of investigating / not asking / not registering mid-way information) has the strongest AI-native, quantified evidence — at least FOUR structurally distinct LLM-benchmark failure structures, several with the key property that the agent HAS the missing-info signal internally yet fails to act on it.
findings.evidence: Structural types: (6a) RECOGNITION-VS-BEHAVIOR DISSOCIATION -> when explicitly asked to JUDGE, models identify ambiguous queries (~60-80% accuracy on the ambiguous class), but in the QA setting they overwhelmingly guess a direct answer (80-95% of responses answer-only) and almost never ask a clarifying question (<=~5% even for the best-performing Claude family without context). The failure is action-not-detection: the agent detects the ambiguity but does not act. Su & Cardie 2026, 'Knowing but Not Showing' (arXiv 2605.25284), 10 models tested [claims 6,7]. Rebuildable as a metric separating latent recognition from overt inquiry. (6b) INJECTED-CONTEXT-SUPPRESSES-INQUIRY -> adding retrieved context makes models EVEN LESS likely to ask clarifying questions, regardless of whether the query is still ambiguous — a false sense of sufficiency; a distinct trigger from baseline under-asking, directly usable as a 'context is a trap' world. Same paper [claim 8]. (6c) INCOMPATIBLE-HYPOTHESIS-SAMPLING + PREMATURE-OVERCONFIDENCE -> naive LLMs in an active info-gathering loop (20 Questions) sample hypotheses incompatible with past observations and grow overconfident, and BOTH failures WORSEN as the interaction history grows — the model does not keep its live hypothesis set consistent with accumulated observations. BED-LLM 2025 (arXiv 2508.21184), GPT-4o baseline [claim 9]; cross-lists with Vice #1 (belief updating) and Vice #5 (long-horizon degradation). (6d) NON-ADAPTIVE-QUESTIONING -> prompting-based LLMs fail to tailor the next question to answers already received; the query-selection policy does not choose the most discriminating next probe (Prompt-Only 45% vs BED-LLM 93% on Animals, ~48pt gap). Same paper [claim 10]. RECENCY CAVEAT: both sources are 2025-2026 preprints (BED-LLM accepted ICLR 2026; Su & Cardie ~2 months old, not yet peer-reviewed) — the mechanisms are robust across models but treat exact percentages as provisional.

findings.claim: Vice #7 (causal-reasoning failures: confounding, correlation-vs-causation) is documented via a clean confounding structure replicated across four independent water-science episodes, plus a distinct corrupt-reference causal variant.
findings.vote: 3-0 on the confounding synthesis [13] and the corrupt-reference case [16]
findings.evidence: Structural types: (7a) IMPROPER-REMOVAL-OF-CONFOUNDS -> a latent nuisance variable (dissolved solutes, dissolved gases, nanobubbles) tracks the manipulation and produces the observed 'effect', which is then misattributed to an exotic cause; the researcher fails to control or intervene on the nuisance. Elton & Spencer 2020 identify this as the PRIMARY shared mechanism across polywater, the Mpemba effect, Pollack's EZ 'fourth phase', and static-magnetic-field-on-water (arXiv 2010.07287) [claim 13] — canonical confounding / correlation-vs-causation, shown four times. (7b) CORRUPT-REFERENCE causal variant -> an erroneous validating measurement leads to the wrong causal conclusion (the true ozone anomaly judged an instrument artifact); Bhartia & McPeters 2018 (S1631071318301196) [claim 16], cross-listed with Vice #1. CAVEATS: the 'four fields' in 7a are four SUB-AREAS within water science, not four separate disciplines; and the explicit observation-vs-intervention framing (the question's collider/selection-bias and 'must intervene when observation is insufficient' sub-structures) is only implicit here — collider/selection-bias cases and interventional-necessity cases were NOT separately confirmed and are a follow-up target.

findings.claim: COVERAGE GAPS (negative finding): no confirmed case survived verification for Vice #4 (failure to infer hidden/latent structure / missing the imaginative leap) or Vice #5 (loss of constraints/goal over long horizons); combined with the single-source thinness of Vice #2, these are the portfolio's structural blind spots.
findings.vote: meta-observation over the full 23-claim set; certain as a statement about THIS evidence, uncertain as a statement about the world
findings.evidence: Across all 23 verified claims, ZERO map cleanly to Vice #4 or Vice #5. Vice #4 (hidden/latent structure, retreat to familiar textbook models, missing the imaginative leap) has no confirmed exemplar — the pathological-water cases are the OPPOSITE error (inventing exotic structure where a mundane confound suffices), and no Dunbar in-vivo analogy case or 'retreat to textbook model' trace was confirmed. Vice #5 (long-horizon goal/constraint loss) has only a tangential signal: BED-LLM's finding that hypothesis-incompatibility and overconfidence 'worsen as history grows' [claim 9] gestures at long-horizon degradation but was classed under Vice #6, and no dedicated agentic long-horizon drift benchmark survived. This absence reflects the search's coverage, not proven non-existence — but for WAGER it means the current case library would let a portfolio over-represent Vices #1/#3/#6/#7 and under-test #4/#5, exactly the overfitting risk the project is trying to avoid. These two vices (plus non-history-of-science structural diversity for Vice #2) should be the explicit target of a follow-up search before world-building.

result.caveats: TAXONOMY-INVERSION CAVEAT (Vice #1, claims [2][3], both 2-1): Oeberst & Imhoff 2023 argue for PARSIMONY — one common mechanism (belief-consistent information processing) with six stage-specific manifestations — which is the OPPOSITE framing from 'six different mechanisms.' At WAGER's world-building granularity the six forms and five stages still map to genuinely different world mechanics, so the structural-variety payload holds, but the report should phrase them as 'stage/form-specific manifestations of one belief-consistent-processing mechanism,' not 'six independent mechanisms.' CROSS-CUTTING VICES: several cases legitimately instantiate two vices — the ozone corrupt-reference case is Vice #1 + #7 [16]; the continental-drift single-gap veto is Vice #1 + #2 [19]; p-hacking is Vice #3 + #6 [21][22]; BED-LLM incompatible-hypothesis sampling is Vice #6 + #1 + #5 [9]. Vice boundaries are not clean partitions, so the same case can seed worlds for different vices depending on what the scoring isolates. REFUTED STRUCTURES NOT TO REBUILD: (a) the ozone 'QC filter silently auto-discards the discovery' structure was REFUTED (1-2) — the NASA TOMS/SBUV principals explicitly rebut it; only the 'corrupt validating reference + demand-corroboration/discount-the-anomaly' structure survived. (b) The generic 'continental drift = judgment error' framing was refuted (1-2) in favor of the sharper acceptance-vs-pursuit and single-gap-veto structural claims that DID survive — use the specific ones. SOURCE-STRENGTH / RECENCY: Vice #6 rests entirely on 2025-2026 preprints (BED-LLM arXiv 2508.21184; Su & Cardie arXiv 2605.25284, ~2 months old, not yet peer-reviewed); the mechanisms are robust and multi-model but exact percentages are provisional. FIELD-DIVERSITY IS UNEVEN: Vice #1 spans formal/lab/physics/earth-science/atmospheric-science; but Vice #7's 'four fields' are four sub-areas of water science (not four disciplines), and Vices #2's three structures all come from ONE source/episode (continental drift) — cross-source, cross-domain corroboration is weak for #2 and moderate for #7. Finally, even within the well-covered Vice #1, the question's structure (a) — 'contradicting evidence arriving mid-investigation that must be incorporated' — is only weakly represented (mainly via LLM history-incorporation failures under Vice #6) and could use a dedicated human/lab exemplar.
openQuestions: Vices #4 (hidden/latent structure, missing the imaginative leap) and #5 (long-horizon goal/constraint loss) produced ZERO confirmed cases — what concrete, quantified episodes exist? Candidate targets for a dedicated search: Dunbar's in-vivo lab studies on analogical distance and 'unexpected findings' handling for #4; agentic long-horizon benchmarks (SWE-bench, GAIA, tau-bench, WebArena) documenting constraint/goal drift over many steps for #5.

For Vice #2, is there structural diversity beyond the single continental-drift episode — e.g., sunk-cost / escalation-of-commitment lab paradigms, N-rays/cold-fusion-style tunnel-vision persistence, or LLM agentic rabbit-holing/loop traces — so a portfolio does not overfit to one historical source?

Do documented AI-agent traces exist for the FORMAL Vice-#1 falsifiability-geometry (Klayman-Ha subset vs superset) — e.g., FALSIFYBENCH or 'Failing to Falsify' LLM results — so the identical structure can be instantiated in both human and LLM subjects and validated as vice-specific rather than substrate-specific?

For the causal vice (#7), the confirmed evidence covers confounding and corrupt-reference, but the question's collider/selection-bias and 'observation-insufficient-so-must-intervene' sub-structures were not separately confirmed — what concrete cases (e.g., Berkson's paradox in medicine, selection-bias case studies, do-calculus/intervention benchmarks) fill those distinct sub-structures?

refuted.claim: STRUCTURE 'QC filter eats the discovery': NASA's SBUV/TOMS ozone-retrieval algorithm was built for total-column ozone in the 200-650 DU range and automatically flagged/rejected values far outside that band as unreliable, which caused large rejection of the anomalously low Antarctic data when the Sept/Oct 1983 SBUV and TOMS data were processed. A synthetic world can rebuild this: an automated quality-control filter treats the genuine extreme signal as out-of-range noise and discards it (bears on vice 3 'failure to verify / accepting a cheap answer' and vice 1 'dismissing anomalous evidence').

Continental drift is a documented JUDGMENT error, not mere ignorance: the paper argues geologists had adequate grounds yet reasoned wrongly, concluding it was RATIONAL to pursue Drift and IRRATIONAL to reject it as unworthy of pursuit (vice #1 dogmatic rejection / vice #2 premature abandonment). This anchors the ~50-year delay (Wegener 1912/1915 -> plate tectonics acceptance early 1960s) as a genuine judgment vice.
